primary_decisions_supported: Identifying hiring needs to hit targets
executive_questions_answered: Do we have enough sales capacity (fully ramped equivalent reps) to hit our revenue targets?
How many new reps (and by when) do we need to hire to achieve next year's bookings plan?
What is our current quota coverage ratio relative to the sales target (e.g., do we have 3x quota vs goal)?

metric_definitions.definition: The ratio of total available sales quota to the sales target for a period. Having 3-4x pipeline or quota coverage is often desired to hit goal (assuming ~25% win rate).
metric_definitions.formula: Coverage = Sum of quotas of all sales reps for period / Sales target for period

known_variants.definition_delta: Adjust quotas for ramp time or current fill (e.g., only count % of quota for unfilled or ramping positions)
known_variants.when_to_use: Mid-year assessments when not all headcount is fully ramped

pitfalls: If win rates differ from assumption, required coverage will differ (e.g., higher win rate means need less coverage and vice versa) – align coverage targets with actual win rate
Overhiring to get very high coverage can waste budget if not needed (diminishing returns beyond a point)
Do not double-count overlapping quotas (if two reps share a quota territory, capacity isn't additive unless both carry full quota separately)

metric_definitions.definition: The number of fully productive sales reps equivalent to the current team, accounting for ramp time and open positions. For example, 5 new hires at 50% productivity = 2.5 FRE.
metric_definitions.formula: FRE = Sum of (Quota capacity of rep / Full quota) across all reps

known_variants.definition_delta: Similar concept, sometimes expressed in terms of full-time equivalents ready to sell at full capacity
known_variants.when_to_use: Communicating capacity to non-sales executives or finance

pitfalls: Assumes an average productivity normalization – individual variances not shown (some reps overperform, some underperform)
Open roles count as 0 until filled – could make capacity look low; optionally can include planned hires as partial FRE if certain
Rapid changes (many hires or attrition) can make FRE fluctuate – consider using averages or exit rate for smooth planning

required_timestamps: as-of date for capacity (usually beginning of quarter)

joins_and_keys.notes: Associate each rep with their assigned quota

transformation_rules: If using annual quotas but looking at quarterly targets, prorate quotas or ensure same period alignment
Apply ramp-up factors: e.g., if a rep is hired 3 months ago with a 6-month ramp, count only 50% of their quota toward capacity
Include planned hires: for forward-looking capacity, can model expected hire dates and ramp contributions

data_quality_checks.logic: Sum of all active quotas should be >= sales target * desired coverage (e.g., 3-4x) – flag if below
All budgeted headcount positions have an entry (filled or open) with corresponding quota in model

derived_fields: Rep_Ramp_Factor = function(months_since_hire) (e.g., 1.0 if > ramp period, else proportion of ramp completed)
aggregation_rules: Sum Effective_Quota of all reps = Total capacity
Fully Ramped Equivalent = Total capacity / Full quota per rep (if quotas differ per rep, use average full quota or treat sum of quotas as already reflecting capacity)
calculations_and_models.forecast_logic: Project revenue capacity by applying historical attainment rates to total capacity (e.g., if team historically achieves 80% of quota on average, factor that in)

layout.purpose: Show total sales quota capacity relative to sales target (coverage ratio)
Detail number of fully ramped equivalent reps and where capacity is coming from (e.g., # of ramping vs full reps)

narrative_insights_to_generate: State our current quota coverage ratio and interpret if it's sufficient given win rates (e.g., 'coverage is 3.5x, likely adequate')
Note if we have a capacity shortfall (e.g., coverage <3x) and need to hire or increase quotas
diagnostic_questions: Do we have enough reps to hit next quarter's target, assuming average productivity?
If we delay planned hires, how will that impact capacity in the second half of the year?

recommended_actions.trigger_condition: Capacity coverage drops below threshold (e.g., <2.5x) for that segment's target
Total quota capacity far exceeds target (e.g., >5x), indicating potential to raise quotas or reduce cost

implementation_notes.minimum_viable_version: Static model calculating current coverage and simple FRE count, updated in spreadsheets periodically
implementation_notes.advanced_version: Interactive capacity planning tool linking to live roster data, allowing scenario planning (e.g., what if we hire X more AEs by Q3) and auto-adjusting ramp contributions
common_client_customizations: Incorporate different productivity assumptions by tenure (e.g., tenured reps exceed quota 110%, new at 50%) rather than uniform ramp factor
Account for attrition: assume some rep turnover in capacity forecast (reduce FRE accordingly over time)
privacy_and_access_notes: Share summary metrics widely, but keep individual quota data within leadership/ops (compensation sensitivity)
If discussing open headcount, be mindful of communication – internal use primarily
success_criteria: No mid-year surprises in needing extra hires – model accurately predicted needs and was followed
Sales targets are met or exceeded with adequate coverage, validating the capacity planning assumptions
